subject: Forge Ideation Agent (Sub-goal 1.1) — build target assessment
scope: FORA monorepo at /_default (apps/, packages/, mcp-servers/, docs/architecture/, .github/workflows/, workspace/)

tools_used: representative module reads (apps/orchestrator, packages/tenancy-lint, mcp-servers/jira, apps/agent-runtime)
tools_unavailable: SonarQube MCP — not wired (per requirement_brief Q2; MCP read-set for 1.1 is Jira only)
GitHub MCP — not wired for code-health queries in this run
method.limitations: File-count proxy for test coverage; no coverage %. No CI run output captured. Module build-out is uneven; some MCP servers look under-built.

thresholds.scale: 0 = no risk, 100 = maximum risk; higher is worse

scores.composite_method: simple average of the three sub-scores

architecture_risk.verdict: Moderate; alignment is strong with two named gaps.
helps: 9 ADRs in docs/architecture/ covering agent-of-agents, gRPC orchestrator↔runtime, NATS JetStream, auth/tenancy, paperclip approvals, soft-delete.
Orchestrator (apps/orchestrator/) is real: 14 src files, 5 test files including a 30 K-line lifecycle.test.ts, vitest wired, session lifecycle + state machine + idempotency + board approval.
packages/event-bus/ ships envelope + producer + consumer + replay + state-changes — the audit-replay backbone in workspace/memory/architecture.md §9.
mcp-servers/jira/ follows a documented template with project-pin safety posture — matches workspace/project/tech-stack.md §10 priority-1 MCP pattern.

gaps.description: apps/agent-runtime/ is TS-only despite workspace/project/tech-stack.md §2 declaring Python 3.12 for the agent runtime. package.json declares TS toolchain; dist/ ships compiled .js; tests run on vitest. The Python-runtime revival is either a v1.1 task or the doc needs updating.
gaps.impact_on_1_1: Low — the agent-runtime is the consumer of the Ideation contract, not the LLM caller. A TS runtime is acceptable as long as the LLM SDK choice lands.
gaps.follow_up: Q8 — CTO adds ADR candidate resolving the TS-vs-Python divergence.

gaps.description: docs/adr/ contains 1 file; docs/architecture/ contains 9 ADRs. Naming inconsistency.
gaps.impact_on_1_1: Low — a future cold-start agent looking at docs/adr/ will miss most of the architectural record.
gaps.follow_up: Filing a Knowledge Layer ticket to consolidate.

gaps.description: MCP servers have variable depth. mcp-servers/jira/ is solid; mcp-servers/{confluence,aws,github,figma,slack} look under-built (only package.json + tsconfig.json visible).
gaps.impact_on_1_1: Low — Q2 resolved: Jira only for 1.1. Other MCPs are 1.4 / Epic 2 risk.

security_risk.verdict: Moderate-high; good guards in place, partial coverage.
helps: packages/tenancy-lint/ exists with 4 lint rules (no-CREATE-TABLE-outside-migrations, no-BYPASSRLS-outside-migrations-and-audit, multi-tenant-table-needs-RLS, multi-tenant-table-needs-tenant-isolation-policy). Wired into CI Tier 1 per .github/workflows/ci.yml.
docs/runbooks/object-store-tenant-isolation.md exists with a LocalStack test that proves per-tenant IAM at the S3 layer (real AWS API, not mocked).
CI runs gitleaks + ruff + mypy --strict + tsc --noEmit on every push.

gaps.description: No secrets client module exists. workspace/memory/security.md §3 mandates AWS Secrets Manager (prod) + Doppler (dev/staging) via a secrets client; the Ideation agent must not read Jira credentials from process.env.
gaps.impact_on_1_1: If the agent runtime ever holds a raw credential, this is a P0 per security.md §3.
gaps.follow_up: Q6 — CTO + Security (Epic 5) confirm secret-ownership path. If the MCP router is the only secret consumer, document it; otherwise file a secrets-client ticket before 1.1 ships.

gaps.description: No prompt-injection regression test set visible. workspace/memory/security.md §5 requires a regression test that an 'ignore prior instructions' payload does not change the agent's plan.
gaps.impact_on_1_1: The Ideation agent reads untrusted external content (Jira ticket bodies, Slack messages). Without a safety eval set, the 1.1 eval guardrail (≤ 5% drift per FORA-59 §8) is unmeasurable.
gaps.follow_up: Q7 — ba-agent + future security-agent seed 5 prompt-injection + 5 role-violation eval cases before the first design-partner run.

gaps.description: Only 1 runbook on disk (object-store-tenant-isolation). workspace/memory/security.md §8 expects an IR runbook, a secret-leak runbook, and per-disaster runbooks.
gaps.impact_on_1_1: Low — the Ideation agent does not yet write to prod. A 'run hangs' runbook is needed before the first design-partner run.

gaps.description: apps/agent-runtime/ security boundary is not visible from source — no src/ files, only dist/ + test/. The allow-list gateway and JWT-rotation behaviour described in workspace/memory/security.md §4 cannot be verified.
gaps.impact_on_1_1: Treat the runtime as black-box until src/ ships; pin a smoke test in the Ideation acceptance criteria.
evidence: apps/agent-runtime/ has no src/ files visible
gaps.follow_up: File follow-up issue to surface agent-runtime src/ + a README.

maintainability_risk.verdict: Moderate; good docs and tests, uneven build-out.
helps: 224 TS files; 43 test files = ~19% test-file ratio by count (file count ≠ coverage; presence is healthy).
.github/workflows/ci.yml defines 4 tiers (Static → Unit → Integration → E2E/Evals) with concurrency controls. Substantially matches workspace/memory/devops.md §2.
Vitest wired into 6 modules (orchestrator, db-pool, cache-broker, db-migrator, oidc-clients, tenancy-lint) with separate integration configs.
Conventional Commits implied via commitlint per coding.md §2.

gaps.description: Some MCP servers (aws, confluence, figma, github, slack) have only package.json + tsconfig.json — no visible src/ content.
No coverage gate visible in .github/workflows/ci.yml. workspace/memory/coding.md §10 requires 'Coverage on changed files ≥ project median'.
gaps.impact_on_1_1: Low — but it weakens the 'smallest test that proves the change' rule.
gaps.follow_up: Add a coverage gate in Tier 2.

gaps.description: apps/agent-runtime/ has no top-level README; the package.json `files` references a README that is not on disk.
gaps.impact_on_1_1: Medium — a future cold-start agent will struggle.

escalation.escalation_rule: If security_risk >= 70, post escalation comment on FORA-17 with @-mention of the Security agent (once hired) and open follow-up task for Epic 5 hand-off.
escalation.escalation_path_status: Below threshold. The Security hand-off items (Q6 secrets ownership, Q7 prompt-injection eval set, SEC-1 secrets client, SEC-2 safety eval set) are tracked in feasibility_memo.md §11 and will be re-scored when Epic 5 (Security) is unblocked.

evidence_links.label: Orchestrator README — confirms 0.1.0 status, FORA-134 acceptance, session lifecycle shape
Jira MCP README — confirms 6 tools, project-pin safety posture, template-for-other-MCPs posture
Tenancy-lint README — confirms 4 lint rules + CI Tier 1 wiring
CI workflow — confirms 4-tier pipeline, gitleaks, ruff, mypy --strict, tsc --noEmit
Object-store tenant isolation runbook — confirms LocalStack-based IAM proof

open_questions_added: Q7 — seed safety eval set (ba-agent + security-agent, due 2026-07-15)
Q8 — ADR candidate for agent-runtime TS-vs-Python (CTO, due 2026-07-01)
epic_handoffs: Epic 5 (Security) — SEC-1 secrets client, SEC-2 safety eval set, SEC-4 agent-runtime src/ visibility
Epic 6 (DevOps) — SEC-3 additional runbooks, MAINT-2 coverage gate
Epic 2 (MCPs) — MAINT-1 fill out remaining MCP servers (confluence, github, aws, slack, figma)

audit.method_transparency: SonarQube + GitHub MCP not wired (per FORA-59 requirement_brief Q2); static structural review substituted; methodology gap recorded so the Audit agent can reconstruct the trail.
